intro: This is the team's ranked to-do list, mission-first — the highest-value work is at the top. Each item shows a priority score: higher means more worth doing now for the effort it takes. Betting work sits at the bottom and is tagged research-only — it is an experiment we measure, never the main product and never the top of the list.

items.title: Build the Coach Dashboard
items.one_liner: A team-first view: matchup roll-ups, per-player skill breakdowns and player values, all from data we already have.

items.title: Build the front-office manager dashboard
items.one_liner: A front-office view: value for the money, a ranked trade board and availability news, with honest coverage flags.

items.title: Build foul trouble into the game simulator
items.one_liner: Fold the finished foul-modeling pieces into the play-by-play game simulation so fouls shape the game directly, and retire the older pre-game point adjustments.

items.title: Make this board clearer and more useful
items.one_liner: Fix how fresh it stays, the daily rollover, the live-work list, and the layout so the board reads at a glance.

items.title: Hold every page to a high design bar
items.one_liner: No blank fields, no arbitrary limits, and every chart measured against the best in its class — on any page you see.

items.title: Make sure tonight's game reports actually send
items.one_liner: Confirm the evening final, the midnight preview, and the pre-tip reminders all fire on time — not just that they are scheduled.

items.title: Show the real, prioritized to-do list here
items.one_liner: Put the team's actual ranked work list on the board so what you see matches what is being worked on next.

items.title: Fix ratings for players with little data
items.one_liner: Adjust how new or low-sample players are rated so their numbers are honest — likely the root cause behind the player-ranking issue.

items.title: Show the team behind the board on its own page
items.one_liner: A page that lays out who does what, so the operation behind every number is visible.

items.title: Send you an alert the moment work finishes
items.one_liner: Wire finished work to your alerts and prove the messages arrive, with a catch-up for today's big items.

items.title: Add an outside-eyes review of how the team works
items.one_liner: An independent advisor audits how the operation runs and where effort is wasted.

items.title: Build the long-form breakdowns page
items.one_liner: A page for written matchup studies and season-trend pieces, designed to a high bar.

items.title: Find and cut wasted effort
items.one_liner: Sweep the operation for redundant work and wasted spend.

items.title: Settle which playing-time projection to trust
items.one_liner: Decide between competing minutes models, tested hardest on games where player roles change.

items.title: Model foul trouble inside the game simulator
items.one_liner: A first building block for tracking foul situations in the play-by-play simulation.

items.title: Make the overnight operation more durable
items.one_liner: A group of fixes so work survives restarts and interruptions cleanly.

items.title: Fold recent rules into the permanent playbook
items.one_liner: Land the latest operating rules into the permanent guide so nothing lives only in a memo.

items.title: Map where our predictions miss
items.one_liner: A diagnostic that breaks down every prediction error to show where the model can improve most.

items.title: Explore where we might beat the betting market
items.one_liner: Look for spots where our calls could beat the market — an experiment, not a product, and never the top priority.
items.tag: Research only — an experiment, never the top priority

items.title: Track how often player-total unders hit
items.one_liner: Watch player-total unders over time — an early experiment we are only measuring.
items.tag: Research only — an experiment, never the top priority

floor.label: 69 things are lined up in the work queue — a healthy runway (we keep at least 40 ready).
